text.title: modern-ba-framework Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for modern-ba-framework. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: Declare one or more perspectives explicitly at project start. Default-Agile routing hides Business Architecture and BPM concerns until late in the project.
rule.rationale: LLMs default to the Agile perspective because it dominates training data; without explicit declaration the agent misses BI, BizArch, and BPM concerns that are equally important for the actual work.

rule.statement: Score competencies against SFIA v9 and certification curricula separately. Certifications signal coverage of a knowledge domain; SFIA scores actual skill capability level.
rule.rationale: A CBAP holder has demonstrated BA knowledge breadth but not necessarily capability with LLMs, RAG, or eval design; conflating the two produces hiring decisions that miss AI-era requirements.

rule.statement: Run a planner subagent that reads project context and emits ba-approach.json before any KA work starts. The planner routes; it does not execute BA work. Human PO/BA sign-off is mandatory before execution.
rule.rationale: Auto-execution skips the only checkpoint where a real PO/BA can correct perspective bias and scope errors before they propagate through the entire pipeline.

rule.statement: Lock model selection per task in the ba-approach.json. Do not allow cost-driven downgrade at runtime.
rule.rationale: Cost regressions happen when orchestrators downgrade from opus to sonnet for gap analysis; the quality difference is significant and the cost saving is marginal relative to the rework cost.
